name: EuiTourStep

children.description: Element to which the tour step popover attaches when open

aria-labelledby.description:
Alternative option to `aria-label` that takes an `id`.
Usually takes the `id` of the popover title
Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.
@see aria-describedby.

container.description: Restrict the popover's position within this element

display.description: CSS display type for both the popover and anchor

offset.description: Distance away from the anchor that the popover will render

ownFocus.description: Traps tab focus within the popover contents

focusTrapProps.description: Object of props passed to EuiFocusTrap

repositionOnScroll.description:
When `true`, the popover's position is re-calculated when the user
scrolls, this supports having fixed-position popover anchors. When nesting
an `EuiPopover` in a scrollable container, `repositionOnScroll` should be `true`

anchorPosition.description: Alignment of the popover and arrow relative to the button

attachToAnchor.description:
Style and position alteration for arrow-less attachment.
Intended for use with inputs as anchors, e.g. EuiInputPopover

hasArrow.description: Show arrow indicating to originating button

initialFocus.description:
Specifies what element should initially have focus; Can be a DOM
node, or a selector string (which will be passed to
document.querySelector() to find the DOM node), or a function that
returns a DOM node.

If not passed, initial focus defaults to the popover panel.

insert.description:
Passed directly to EuiPortal for DOM positioning. Both properties are
required if prop is specified

panelClassName.description: Custom class added to the EuiPanel containing the popover contents

panelStyle.description: Standard DOM `style` attribute. Passed to the EuiPanel

panelProps.description: Object of props passed to EuiPanel. See {@link EuiPopoverPanelProps}

popoverScreenReaderText.description:
Optional screen reader instructions to announce upon popover open,
in addition to EUI's default popover instructions for Escape on close.
Useful for popovers that may have additional keyboard capabilities such as
arrow navigation.

repositionToCrossAxis.description:
By default, popovers will attempt to position themselves along the initial
axis specified. If there is not enough room either vertically or horizontally
however, the popover will attempt to reposition itself along the secondary
cross axis if there is room there instead.

If you do not want this repositioning to occur (and it is acceptable for
the popover to appear offscreen), set this to false to disable this behavior.

zIndex.description:
By default, popover content inherits the z-index of the anchor
component; pass `zIndex` to override

buffer.description:
Minimum distance between the popover and the bounding container;
Pass an array of 4 values to adjust each side differently: `[top, right, bottom, left]`

arrowChildren.description:
Element to pass as the child element of the arrow;
Use case is typically limited to an accompanying `EuiBeacon`

onPositionChange.description: Function callback for when the popover positon changes

closePopover.description: Callback to handle hiding of the popover

anchor.description: Selector or reference to the element to which the tour step popover attaches when open

content.description: Contents of the tour step popover

isStepOpen.description: Step will display if set to `true`

minWidth.description: Change the default min width of the popover panel

maxWidth.description: Change the default max width of the popover panel

onFinish.description: Function to call for 'Skip tour' and 'End tour' actions

step.description: The number of the step within the parent tour. 1-based indexing.

stepsTotal.description: The total number of steps in the tour

subtitle.description: Smaller title text that appears atop each step in the tour. The subtitle gets wrapped in the appropriate heading level.

title.description: Larger title text specific to this step. The title gets wrapped in the appropriate heading level.

decoration.description:
Extra visual indication of step location.
It does not apply when `hasArrow=false`

footerAction.description:
Accepts any `ReactNode` to replace the 'Skip tour' link in the footer.
Ideally, pass one button or an array of up to 2 buttons.

example: <EuiTourStep title="Welcome!" content="Tour step content" isStepOpen stepsTotal={3}>Welcome!</EuiTourStep>
